document: Guidelines on Outsourcing Financial Institutions Other than Banks updated (Singapore - Row 17)

sections.title: Outsourcing risks and regulatory obligations for financial institutions
sections.summary: Financial institutions must maintain robust risk management frameworks for outsourcing arrangements to mitigate reputation, compliance, operational, security, and country risks. The institution remains fully responsible for legal and regulatory compliance regardless of service provider location or outsourcing scope. Institutions must self-assess existing outsourcing arrangements against MAS guidelines and address concentration risks from single providers.
sections.dtia_summary: Outsourcing arrangements require risk management frameworks to address country and concentration risks when service providers are overseas. This section mandates specific risk controls for financial institutions.

requirements.text: Institutions must adopt a sound and responsive risk management framework for outsourcing arrangements
Institutions must conduct self-assessment of existing outsourcing arrangements against MAS guidelines

sections.title: Board and senior management oversight of outsourcing risk governance
sections.summary: The board and senior management must maintain oversight of outsourcing risks, including establishing risk appetite, approval authorities, and governance structures. They must ensure comprehensive risk assessment, mitigation, and regular reviews of outsourcing arrangements to maintain institutional soundness and regulatory compliance. This applies to all financial institutions supervised by MAS, with specific responsibilities for board committees and local management of foreign entities.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates robust governance frameworks for outsourcing risk management, requiring institutions to assess and mitigate cross-border outsourcing risks through board oversight, risk appetite setting, and regular reviews of service providers. Critical for DTIAs involving international service providers.

requirements.text: Board must approve framework for evaluating risks and materiality of outsourcing arrangements
Board must set risk appetite for outsourcing arrangements
Board must establish approval authorities for outsourcing arrangements
Board must assess management competencies for outsourcing risk policies
Board must ensure senior management establishes governance structures for risk management
Board must review outsourcing strategies for continued relevance and safety
Senior management must evaluate materiality and risks of outsourcing arrangements
Senior management must develop sound outsourcing policies and procedures
Senior management must review effectiveness of policies and adjust as needed
Senior management must monitor risks from material outsourcing arrangements
Senior management must ensure contingency plans are in place and tested
Senior management must ensure independent review and audit of outsourcing compliance
Senior management must take timely remedial actions for audit findings
Senior management must communicate outsourcing risks to the board
Board must establish communication procedures with delegated committees
Local management of foreign institutions must comply with Singaporean laws

sections.source_quote: The board and senior management of an institution play pivotal roles in ensuring a sound risk management culture and environment. While an institution may delegate day-to-day operational duties to the service provider, the responsibilities for maintaining effective oversight and governance of outsourcing arrangements, managing outsourcing risks, and implementing an adequate outsourcing risk management framework, in accordance with these Guidelines, continue to rest with the institution, its board and senior management.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Business Continuity for Outsourced Services
sections.summary: Financial institutions must proactively verify service providers' business continuity plans, including regular testing to meet recovery objectives. Institutions require service providers to notify them of any tests affecting performance, substantial BCP changes, or adverse developments. Institutions must also ensure procedures exist to address termination of outsourcing arrangements, enabling business continuity and secure removal of data from service providers.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires institutions to validate service providers' business continuity capabilities and ensure data can be recovered if outsourcing ends, critical for cross-border data transfers involving third parties.

requirements.text: Proactively seek assurance on the state of BCP preparedness of the service provider
Ensure service provider regularly tests BCP to meet recovery objectives
Require service provider to notify of any test findings affecting performance
Require service provider to notify of substantial BCP changes
Require service provider to notify of adverse developments impacting service
Ensure plans exist to address termination of outsourcing arrangements
Ensure data can be promptly removed or destroyed from service provider

sections.source_quote: Proactively seek assurance on the state of BCP preparedness of the service provider. It should ensure the service provider regularly tests its BCP to ascertain that the recovery objectives can be met.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Material outsourcing arrangements for financial institutions
sections.summary: Financial institutions must identify and assess material outsourcing arrangements based on factors including business impact, financial risk, reputation, customer impact, cost implications, and service provider reliability. Materiality is determined by the institution's ability to maintain internal controls and regulatory compliance if the service provider fails. Institutions must conduct periodic reviews to detect new outsourcing risks as services or volumes change.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires financial institutions to evaluate material outsourcing arrangements to ensure data transfers and processing activities meet regulatory safeguards, particularly for high-risk services like risk management and internal controls.

requirements.text: An institution should assess materiality in outsourcing arrangements considering business impact, financial risk, reputation, customer impact, cost, and service provider reliability
Outsourcing all or substantially all risk management or internal control functions, including compliance, internal audit, financial accounting and actuarial, is material
Institutions should undertake periodic reviews of outsourcing arrangements to identify new risks from incremental services, increased volume, or service provider changes
An institution should consider materiality at both institutional and group levels (branches and corporations)

sections.source_quote: An institution should assess the materiality in an outsourcing arrangement. In assessing materiality, MAS recognises that qualitative judgment is involved and the circumstances faced by individual institutions may vary.
